name: Core JavaScript and TypeScript — Essential
description: Curated full-stack essentials selected from the complete pack. Use this as a lighter replacement for the full version.

topics.name: Closures
topics.description: Functions retaining access to variables from their lexical environment.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A closure exists when a function keeps access to bindings from the lexical environment where it was created.

## What problem does it solve?

Closures enable private state, callbacks, factories, and memoization, but long-lived closures can retain stale state or large object graphs.

## How it works

- Nested functions can use outer bindings after the outer call returns.
- Each factory call can create separate private state.
- Closures retain bindings, not frozen snapshots.
- Timers, listeners, and caches need lifecycle cleanup.

## Common mistakes

- Saying a closure copies values at creation time.
- Keeping listeners alive after their owner is gone.
- Accidentally sharing one mutable binding between callbacks.

## Key terms

- lexical environment — visible bindings at creation
- retained reference — reference keeping data alive
- stale closure — callback using older logical state
- factory function — function creating configured functions or objects

hu.name: Closures
hu.description: A function később is hozzáfér a létrehozási lexical environment bindingjaihoz.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Closure esetén a function a létrehozási lexical environment változóihoz később is hozzáfér.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Private state, callback, factory és memoization építhető rá, de stale state-et vagy nagy object graphet is életben tarthat.

## Hogyan működik?

- A nested function az outer call befejezése után is használhat outer bindingot.
- Minden factory call külön private state-et hozhat létre.
- A closure bindingot tart elérhetően, nem frozen snapshotot.
- Timer, listener és cache esetén cleanup kellhet.

## Gyakori hibák

- Azt mondani, hogy a closure lemásolja az értéket.
- Listenerrel unmount után is state-et életben tartani.
- Több callback között véletlenül ugyanazt a mutable bindingot megosztani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- lexical environment — a létrehozási helyen látható bindingok
- retained reference — data-t életben tartó reference
- stale closure — régebbi logical state-et olvasó callback
- factory function — konfigurált functiont vagy objectet készítő function

topics.name: Primitive Values and Object References
topics.description: How assignments and function calls behave for primitives and objects.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Primitives are immutable values. Objects are reached through references. Assignment always copies a value; for an object that value is a reference.

## What problem does it solve?

The phrase “passed by reference” is misleading: a function cannot replace the caller variable, but it can mutate the shared object reached through the copied reference.

## How it works

- Primitive assignment copies the primitive.
- Object assignment copies a reference to the same object.
- Parameter reassignment changes only the local binding.
- Property mutation changes the shared object.
- Object equality compares identity.

## Common mistakes

- Expecting equal-looking object literals to be `===`.
- Mutating through an unexpected alias.
- Assuming spread creates a deep copy.

## Key terms

- primitive — immutable non-object value
- reference — value identifying an object
- alias — another reference to the same object
- identity — whether two references point to the same object

hu.name: Primitive Values és Object References
hu.description: Assignment mindig value-t másol; object esetén ez egy reference ugyanahhoz az objecthez.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A primitive immutable value. Objecthez reference-en keresztül férünk hozzá. Assignment mindig value-t másol; object esetén ez a reference.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A „pass by reference” félrevezető: a function nem cseréli le a caller variable-jét, de a copied reference-en keresztül módosíthatja a shared objectet.

## Hogyan működik?

- Primitive assignment a primitive value-t másolja.
- Object assignment ugyanahhoz az objecthez tartozó reference-et másol.
- Parameter reassignment csak local bindingot változtat.
- Property mutation a shared objectet módosítja.
- Object equality identityt hasonlít.

## Gyakori hibák

- Equal-looking object literalnál `===` eredményt várni.
- Váratlan aliason keresztül módosítani.
- Spread után deep copyt feltételezni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- primitive — immutable, nem object value
- reference — objectet azonosító value
- alias — másik reference ugyanahhoz az objecthez
- identity — ugyanarra az objectre mutat-e két reference

topics.name: Immutability, Shallow Copy, and Deep Copy
topics.description: Changing shared state through new values while understanding copy depth.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Immutability means producing a new value instead of changing shared state in place. Spread and `Object.assign` normally create only shallow copies.

## What problem does it solve?

In-place mutation breaks reference-based change detection and makes debugging harder, while blind deep cloning is expensive and may lose semantics.

## How it works

- Copy only the path that changes.
- Use non-mutating array operations when ownership is shared.
- `const` freezes the binding, not object contents.
- `structuredClone` is useful only when its supported semantics fit the data.
- Define clear ownership instead of banning all local mutation.

## Common mistakes

- Believing spread is a deep copy.
- Believing `const` freezes an object.
- Using JSON round-trips for Dates, Maps, cycles, or custom prototypes.

## Key terms

- immutable update — create a new changed value
- shallow copy — copy one level of references
- deep copy — recursively independent nested data
- structural sharing — reuse unchanged subtrees

hu.name: Immutability, Shallow Copy és Deep Copy
hu.description: Shared state változtatásakor új value készül, miközben a copy depth ismert marad.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Immutability esetén shared state in-place módosítása helyett új value készül. A spread és `Object.assign` általában csak shallow copyt ad.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Az in-place mutation elrontja a reference-based change detectiont és nehezíti a debuggingot, a vak deep clone viszont drága és semantics-et veszíthet.

## Hogyan működik?

- Csak a changed pathot másold.
- Shared ownershipnál non-mutating array operationt használj.
- A `const` bindingot rögzít, nem object contentet.
- `structuredClone` csak megfelelő data semantics mellett jó.
- Clear ownership jobb, mint minden local mutation tiltása.

## Gyakori hibák

- A spreadet deep copyként kezelni.
- Azt hinni, hogy `const` lefagyasztja az objectet.
- JSON round-tripet használni Date, Map, cycle vagy custom prototype esetén.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- immutable update — új changed value készítése
- shallow copy — egy reference-szint másolása
- deep copy — nested data független másolata
- structural sharing — unchanged subtree-k újrafelhasználása

topics.name: JavaScript Event Loop
topics.description: Coordination of the call stack, tasks, microtasks, host APIs, and rendering.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

The event loop coordinates synchronous JavaScript with asynchronous work handled by the browser or Node host environment.

## What problem does it solve?

It explains Promise timing, timer ordering, UI freezes, and Node event-loop lag. Async syntax does not make CPU-heavy work parallel.

## How it works

- Run synchronous code until the stack is empty.
- Drain microtasks such as Promise reactions.
- Take the next task such as a timer or event.
- Browsers may render between task turns.
- Split or offload CPU-heavy work.

## Common mistakes

- Saying `setTimeout(fn, 0)` runs immediately.
- Assuming `await` moves CPU work to another thread.
- Creating endless microtask chains that starve other work.

## Key terms

- call stack — executing JavaScript frames
- task/macrotask — queued host event
- microtask — high-priority continuation
- event-loop lag — delay before queued work runs

hu.name: JavaScript Event Loop
hu.description: A call stack, tasks, microtasks és host API-k együttműködését koordinálja.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Az event loop a synchronous JavaScript és a browser vagy Node host environment asynchronous munkáját koordinálja.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ez magyarázza a Promise timingot, timer orderinget, UI freeze-t és Node event-loop lagot. Az async syntax nem teszi parallelé a CPU-heavy worköt.

## Hogyan működik?

- Synchronous code fut, amíg a stack ki nem ürül.
- Ezután lefutnak a microtaskok, például Promise reactionök.
- Majd jön a következő task, például timer vagy event.
- Browserben task turnök között lehet rendering.
- CPU-heavy worköt fel kell darabolni vagy offloadolni.

## Gyakori hibák

- Azt mondani, hogy `setTimeout(fn, 0)` azonnal fut.
- Azt hinni, hogy `await` más threadre teszi a CPU worköt.
- Endless microtask chaint készíteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- call stack — aktuálisan futó frame-ek
- task/macrotask — queued host event
- microtask — high-priority continuation
- event-loop lag — queued work extra késése

topics.name: Promises and async/await
topics.description: Representing asynchronous completion, failure propagation, and readable control flow.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A Promise represents future fulfillment or rejection. `async` functions return Promises, and `await` suspends that function continuation without blocking the thread.

## What problem does it solve?

Promises replace deeply nested callbacks with composable sequencing and predictable error propagation.

## How it works

- Return Promises instead of starting hidden work.
- Await only when later work depends on the result.
- Rejections propagate until handled.
- `try/catch` handles awaited rejections in its scope.
- Decide whether work is awaited, returned, aggregated, or intentionally detached.

## Common mistakes

- Forgetting to return a Promise from a chain callback.
- Catching and silently swallowing errors.
- Sequentially awaiting independent work.

## Key terms

- fulfillment — successful completion
- rejection — failed completion
- continuation — code resumed after await
- unhandled rejection — rejection without an effective handler

hu.name: Promises és async/await
hu.description: Asynchronous completiont, failure propagationt és olvasható control flow-t kezel.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Promise future fulfillmentet vagy rejectiont reprezentál. Az `async` function Promise-t ad vissza, az `await` pedig a function continuationjét függeszti fel thread blocking nélkül.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A Promise composable sequencinget és kiszámítható error propagationt ad a nested callbackek helyett.

## Hogyan működik?

- Hidden work helyett returnölj Promise-t.
- Csak akkor awaitelj, ha a következő lépés függ az eredménytől.
- A rejection továbbterjed, amíg valaki kezeli.
- `try/catch` az adott scope awaited rejectionjét kezeli.
- Legyen tudatos, hogy a work awaited, returned, aggregated vagy detached.

## Gyakori hibák

- Chain callbackből elfelejteni returnölni a Promise-t.
- Errort catch után csendben lenyelni.
- Independent worköt sequential awaitelni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- fulfillment — sikeres completion
- rejection — sikertelen completion
- continuation — await után folytatódó code
- unhandled rejection — handler nélkül maradó rejection

topics.name: Asynchronous Composition and Concurrency Control
topics.description: Combining independent work while controlling failure semantics and resource pressure.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

`Promise.all`, `allSettled`, `race`, and `any` combine asynchronous outcomes. Concurrency limits bound how many operations are in flight.

## What problem does it solve?

Sequential awaits waste time, but unbounded concurrency can overload APIs, pools, browsers, or databases.

## How it works

- Use `Promise.all` when all results are required and fail-fast is correct.
- Use `allSettled` when every outcome must be inspected.
- Use `race` for the first settled result and `any` for the first fulfillment.
- Limit concurrency for large collections.
- Cancellation requires explicit support such as `AbortController`.

## Common mistakes

- Believing `Promise.all` cancels remaining work.
- Running unbounded `map(async ...)` on large input.
- Adding timeouts without aborting underlying work.

## Key terms

- fail fast — group fails on first rejection
- concurrency limit — maximum in-flight work
- cancellation — stopping irrelevant work
- aggregation — combining outcomes

hu.name: Asynchronous Composition és Concurrency Control
hu.description: Independent async work futtatása megfelelő failure semantics és resource limit mellett.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A `Promise.all`, `allSettled`, `race` és `any` több async outcome-ot kapcsol össze. A concurrency limit az egyszerre in-flight work mennyiségét korlátozza.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A sequential await lassú lehet, az unbounded concurrency pedig túlterhelhet API-t, poolt, browsert vagy database-t.

## Hogyan működik?

- `Promise.all`, ha minden result required és fail-fast kell.
- `allSettled`, ha minden outcome-ot külön meg kell nézni.
- `race` az első settled, `any` az első fulfilled resultot adja.
- Nagy collectionnél concurrency limit kell.
- Cancellation explicit támogatást igényel, például `AbortController`.

## Gyakori hibák

- Azt hinni, hogy `Promise.all` cancelöli a többi worköt.
- Nagy inputra unbounded `map(async ...)` futtatni.
- Timeoutot tenni underlying abort nélkül.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- fail fast — első rejectionnél group failure
- concurrency limit — maximum in-flight work
- cancellation — irreleváns work leállítása
- aggregation — outcome-ok összekapcsolása

topics.name: TypeScript strictness, any, unknown, and never
topics.description: Using the type system to expose uncertainty instead of bypassing it.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Strict TypeScript enables stronger checks. `any` disables checking, `unknown` requires narrowing, and `never` represents impossible states or non-returning paths.

## What problem does it solve?

A codebase can compile while unsafe data spreads through `any`. Strictness makes missing cases and nullability visible earlier.

## How it works

- Use strict mode for new code and migrate legacy areas incrementally.
- Accept untrusted values as `unknown`.
- Isolate unavoidable `any` at compatibility boundaries.
- Use `never` for exhaustiveness.
- Model null and undefined explicitly.

## Common mistakes

- Replacing compiler errors with `any`.
- Treating `unknown` as inconvenient `any`.
- Assuming TypeScript validates runtime data.
- Overusing non-null assertions.

## Key terms

- strict mode — stronger compiler checks
- any — opt-out from checking
- unknown — requires narrowing
- never — impossible value or path

hu.name: TypeScript strictness, `any`, `unknown` és `never`
hu.description: A type system segítségével láthatóvá teszi a bizonytalanságot.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A strict TypeScript erősebb checkeket kapcsol be. Az `any` kikapcsolja az ellenőrzést, az `unknown` narrowingot követel, a `never` pedig impossible state-et vagy pathot jelöl.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A codebase compile-olhat úgy is, hogy unsafe data `any`-n keresztül terjed. A strictness korábban láthatóvá teszi a missing case-t és nullabilityt.

## Hogyan működik?

- Új code-nál strict mode, legacy migrationnál fokozatos bevezetés.
- Untrusted value `unknown` legyen.
- Elkerülhetetlen `any` izolált compatibility boundaryn maradjon.
- Exhaustiveness checkhez `never` használható.
- `null` és `undefined` legyen explicit.

## Gyakori hibák

- Compiler errort `any` segítségével eltüntetni.
- Az `unknown`-t kényelmetlen `any`-nak tekinteni.
- Runtime validationt várni TypeScripttől.
- Non-null assertiont túlhasználni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- strict mode — erősebb compiler checkek
- any — type checking kikapcsolása
- unknown — narrowingot igényel
- never — impossible value vagy path

topics.name: TypeScript and Runtime Validation
topics.description: Validating untrusted data before treating it as a typed internal value.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

TypeScript types disappear at compile time. HTTP, storage, queues, environment variables, and third-party data still require runtime validation.

## What problem does it solve?

Casting JSON to an interface only silences the compiler; it does not prove the data is valid.

## How it works

- Accept external data as `unknown`.
- Validate shape, types, ranges, formats, and cross-field rules.
- Map validated DTOs into internal domain models.
- Return structured boundary errors.
- Version and test schemas as contracts.

## Common mistakes

- Using `as SomeType` as validation.
- Validating HTTP but trusting queues or environment variables.
- Sharing one type across DTO, persistence, and domain layers.

## Key terms

- runtime validation — checking values while the program runs
- DTO — boundary data shape
- schema — machine-readable validation contract
- type assertion — compiler instruction with no runtime check

hu.name: TypeScript és Runtime Validation
hu.description: Untrusted data ellenőrzése a typed internal domainbe kerülés előtt.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A TypeScript type compile során eltűnik. HTTP-ből, storage-ból, queue-ból, environment variable-ből vagy third-party source-ból érkező data runtime validationt igényel.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

JSON interface-re castolása csak a compilert hallgattatja el; a data validityjét nem bizonyítja.

## Hogyan működik?

- External data `unknown` legyen.
- Validáld a shape-et, type-ot, range-et, formatot és cross-field rule-t.
- Validated DTO-ból készíts internal domain modelt.
- Structured boundary errort adj.
- A schema contract, ezért versionölni és tesztelni kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- `as SomeType` használata validation helyett.
- HTTP-t validálni, queue-t vagy environment variable-t vakon elhinni.
- Ugyanazt a type-ot használni DTO, persistence és domain layeren.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- runtime validation — real value ellenőrzése futás közben
- DTO — boundary data shape
- schema — machine-readable validation contract
- type assertion — runtime check nélküli compiler instruction

questions.title: Closure state and lifecycle
questions.prompt: A callback continues to read old state and also keeps a large object alive after the feature is closed. Explain how closures can cause both problems and how you would fix them.
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain lexical capture, distinguish stale logical state from retained memory, then describe cleanup or a clearer data flow.

expectedPoints.label: Explains lexical capture
expectedPoints.description: The callback keeps access to bindings from its creation environment; it does not automatically receive fresh logical state.

expectedPoints.label: Explains stale state
expectedPoints.description: Identifies an older render or logical state and suggests explicit parameters, correct dependencies, or a current reference.

expectedPoints.label: Explains retained memory
expectedPoints.description: Recognizes that listeners, timers, or caches can keep the closure and its referenced object graph reachable.

expectedPoints.label: Adds lifecycle cleanup
expectedPoints.description: Removes listeners, cancels timers, clears caches, or restructures ownership when the feature closes.

followUps.prompt: Give a positive example where a closure is the cleanest design.

hu.title: Closure state és lifecycle
hu.prompt: Egy callback régi state-et olvas, és a feature bezárása után is életben tart egy nagy objectet. Hogyan okozhat closure mindkét problémát, és hogyan javítanád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Lexical capture, stale logical state és retained memory szétválasztása, majd cleanup vagy tisztább data flow.

js_closure_capture.label: Elmagyarázza a lexical capture-t
js_closure_capture.description: A callback a létrehozási environment bindingjaihoz fér hozzá; nem kap automatikusan friss logical state-et.

js_closure_stale.label: Elmagyarázza a stale state-et
js_closure_stale.description: Régi render vagy logical state esetén explicit parameter, megfelelő dependency vagy current reference kerül elő.

js_closure_memory.label: Elmagyarázza a retained memoryt
js_closure_memory.description: Listener, timer vagy cache életben tarthatja a closure-t és a hozzá tartozó object graphet.

js_closure_cleanup.label: Lifecycle cleanupot ad
js_closure_cleanup.description: Listener removal, timer cancellation, cache clearing vagy ownership átalakítás szerepel.

followUps.js_closure_followup_factory: Mondj pozitív példát, amikor closure a legtisztább design.

questions.title: A shallow copy still changes the original
questions.prompt: A developer copies an object with spread, changes a nested field, and unexpectedly changes the original state too. Explain why and propose a safe update.
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain reference aliasing and shallow depth, then copy only the changed path.

expectedPoints.label: Explains shallow copy depth
expectedPoints.description: Spread creates a new top-level object but nested references remain shared.

expectedPoints.label: Explains aliasing
expectedPoints.description: Both outer objects still reach the same nested object.

expectedPoints.label: Proposes a path copy
expectedPoints.description: Creates new objects along the changed path and reuses unchanged subtrees.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids blind deep cloning
expectedPoints.description: Notes the cost and semantic limitations of full deep clones.

followUps.prompt: When is direct mutation acceptable and simpler?

hu.title: A shallow copy mégis módosítja az original state-et
hu.prompt: A developer spreaddel másol egy objectet, módosít egy nested fieldet, és az original state is megváltozik. Miért, és mi a safe update?
hu.answerStructureHint: Shared reference és shallow depth, majd csak a changed path másolása.

js_mut_shallow.label: Elmagyarázza a shallow copy depth-et
js_mut_shallow.description: A spread új top-level objectet készít, de a nested reference továbbra is közös.

js_mut_alias.label: Elmagyarázza az aliasingot
js_mut_alias.description: Mindkét outer object ugyanazt a nested objectet éri el.

js_mut_update.label: Path copyt javasol
js_mut_update.description: A changed path mentén készít új objecteket, az unchanged subtree-ket reuse-olja.

js_mut_tradeoff.label: Nem javasol vak deep clone-t
js_mut_tradeoff.description: Megemlíti a full deep clone costját és semantic limitationjeit.

followUps.js_mut_followup_ownership: Mikor elfogadható és egyszerűbb a direct mutation?

questions.title: The UI freezes despite async syntax
questions.prompt: A browser function is declared async but performs a large synchronous transformation before its first await. The page freezes. Explain why and how you would redesign it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate async syntax from parallel CPU work, then discuss chunking, workers, and measurement.

expectedPoints.label: Explains synchronous execution before await
expectedPoints.description: The function runs synchronously on the main thread until an actual suspension point.

expectedPoints.label: Explains that async is not parallel CPU execution
expectedPoints.description: CPU-heavy work still blocks the event loop.

expectedPoints.label: Proposes an appropriate redesign
expectedPoints.description: Moves work to a Web Worker or splits it into smaller tasks.

expectedPoints.label: Measures the effect
expectedPoints.description: Uses long-task or performance profiling instead of guessing.

followUps.prompt: Why can repeatedly scheduling more microtasks still starve rendering?

hu.title: Az UI async syntax mellett is lefagy
hu.prompt: Egy browser function `async`, de az első `await` előtt nagy synchronous transformationt futtat. A page lefagy. Miért, és hogyan terveznéd át?
hu.answerStructureHint: Async syntax és parallel CPU work szétválasztása, majd chunking, worker és measurement.

js_loop_sync.label: Elmagyarázza az `await` előtti synchronous futást
js_loop_sync.description: A function az első valódi suspension pointig main threaden fut.

js_loop_cpu.label: Elmagyarázza, hogy async nem parallel CPU execution
js_loop_cpu.description: A CPU-heavy work továbbra is blokkolja az event loopot.

js_loop_fix.label: Megfelelő redesign-t javasol
js_loop_fix.description: Web Workerbe teszi, vagy kisebb taskokra bontja a worköt.

js_loop_measure.label: Méri a hatást
js_loop_measure.description: Performance profile, long-task vagy responsiveness signal alapján dönt.

followUps.js_loop_followup_microtask: Miért starvingolhatja a renderinget a folyamatosan új microtaskot schedule-ölő code?

questions.title: Fetching several independent dependencies
questions.prompt: A page needs data from four independent APIs. Sometimes one optional API fails. Compare sequential await, Promise.all, and Promise.allSettled, then choose a design.
questions.answerStructureHint: Start with dependency relationships, then choose concurrency and failure semantics for required versus optional data.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies independent work
expectedPoints.description: Runs independent calls concurrently instead of waiting sequentially.

expectedPoints.label: Explains Promise.all
expectedPoints.description: Uses Promise.all when every result is required and fail-fast semantics are correct.

expectedPoints.label: Explains allSettled
expectedPoints.description: Uses allSettled or separate handling when optional results may fail.

expectedPoints.label: Mentions cancellation and timeout
expectedPoints.description: Notes that group rejection does not cancel underlying work.

followUps.prompt: How would the design change if there were 5,000 items instead of four APIs?

hu.title: Több independent dependency lekérése
hu.prompt: Egy page négy independent API adatait használja. Egy optional API néha elbukik. Hasonlítsd össze a sequential await, `Promise.all` és `Promise.allSettled` megoldást, majd válassz designt.
hu.answerStructureHint: Dependency relationship, concurrency és failure semantics required és optional data esetén.

js_prom_independent.label: Felismeri az independent worköt
js_prom_independent.description: Az independent callokat concurrent futtatja, nem sequential awaiteli.

js_prom_all.label: Elmagyarázza a `Promise.all` működését
js_prom_all.description: Akkor használja, ha minden result required és fail-fast kell.

js_prom_settled.label: Elmagyarázza az `allSettled` használatát
js_prom_settled.description: Optional failure esetén minden outcome-ot külön vizsgál, és megtartja a required data-t.

js_prom_cancel.label: Megemlíti a cancellationt és timeoutot
js_prom_cancel.description: A group rejection nem cancelöli az underlying requesteket.

followUps.js_prom_followup_limit: Hogyan változna a design, ha négy API helyett 5000 itemet kellene feldolgozni?

questions.title: TypeScript at an API boundary
questions.prompt: A team parses external JSON and immediately casts it to an internal interface. Why is this unsafe, and how would you design the boundary?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain type erasure, accept unknown, validate the external DTO, then map into the domain model.

expectedPoints.label: Explains type erasure
expectedPoints.description: TypeScript types and assertions do not validate runtime JSON.

expectedPoints.label: Accepts untrusted data as unknown
expectedPoints.description: Uses unknown rather than any so values must be narrowed.

expectedPoints.label: Adds runtime validation
expectedPoints.description: Checks shape, types, formats, ranges, and cross-field rules.

expectedPoints.label: Separates DTO and domain model
expectedPoints.description: Maps validated boundary data into an internal model.

followUps.prompt: Should responses leaving your service also be runtime-validated? When is that worth the cost?

hu.title: TypeScript egy API boundaryn
hu.prompt: A team external JSON-t parse-ol, majd azonnal internal interface-re castolja. Miért unsafe, és hogyan terveznéd meg a boundaryt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Type erasure, `unknown`, external DTO runtime validation, majd domain mapping.

ts_bound_erasure.label: Elmagyarázza a type erasure-t
ts_bound_erasure.description: A TypeScript type és assertion runtime JSON-t nem validál; csak compile-time segítség.

ts_bound_schema.label: Runtime validationt ad
ts_bound_schema.description: Shape, type, format, range és cross-field rule ellenőrzött.

ts_bound_mapping.label: Elkülöníti a DTO-t és domain modelt
ts_bound_mapping.description: Validated boundary data-ból internal model készül.

followUps.ts_bound_followup_output: A service-ből kifelé menő response-t is runtime-validálnád? Mikor éri meg?
